chapter_title.ar: باب بَيْعِ الْعَدَدِ وَ الْمُجَازَفَةِ وَ الشَّيْ‏ءِ الْمُبْهَمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 82 – Selling the numbered, and the estimated, and the vague things

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/210)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/210)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: إِذَا كَانَتْ أَجَمَةٌ لَيْسَ فِيهَا قَصَبٌ أُخْرِجَ شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنَ السَّمَكِ فَيُبَاعُ وَ مَا فِي الْأَجَمَةِ .

en.hubeali: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If there is a grove wherein there are no reeds, something from the fish having been taken out, so whatever is in the grove can be sold’.
en.sarwar: 11. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If there is a bunch of trees without reeds with a few fish taken out along with what is in the bunch of trees, it can (lawfully) be sold.’”
